sections.opening: Come sit beside me, dear child. We have reached the Strength card — a card that perhaps more people misunderstand than any other. Everyone thinks strength means muscles and shouting, but this card teaches us something much deeper and more beautiful. Instead of the Emperor's scepter and sword, here we have a gentle woman opening a lion's mouth without any physical force. This is courage of the soul, of the heart. In the previous lesson about the Hierophant we learned about wisdom that comes from tradition — here we'll learn about wisdom that comes from inner courage. True strength is not conquering our surroundings, but conquering ourselves.
sections.theoryA:
What is the true strength in the Strength card?

The true strength in this card is self-control and inner power expressed through gentleness. The woman in the card doesn't fight the lion — she nurtures it, caresses it, shows it love. This is the strength of love conquering fear, of patience conquering anger, of compassion conquering aggression.

The card's number 8 represents perfect balance between spirit and matter, between body and soul. Unlike the Emperor card which uses external force and authority, Strength teaches us about inner power — the kind that allows us to face life's most difficult challenges without losing our humanity.

Ancient cultures understood that the greatest strength is the ability to control our inner impulses. The lion in the card represents our wild nature — anger, fear, desires. And the woman represents the higher part of us that knows how to work with these forces instead of against them.

When the Strength card appears in a reading, it invites us to look inward and ask: Where do I need patience now instead of brute force? Where do I need to control myself instead of controlling others? This is a card of gentle courage — the bravery to be compassionate even when the world demands you be harsh.

practice1.instruction: Take the Strength card from your deck and observe it for two minutes. Notice the facial expressions of both the woman and the lion. How does she touch him? How does he respond? Write in one word what emotion you see in her face.

sections.theoryB:
How does Strength connect to astrology and Kabbalah?

In astrological connections, Strength connects to Leo — the fire sign representing creativity, pride, and personal power. But notice the paradox: the most "egotistical" sign receives a card that speaks of self-control. This teaches us that even the strongest fire needs to be properly directed.

In the Tree of Life, Strength is found on the path connecting Geburah (strength) to Chesed (mercy). This is exactly what the card teaches — how to take our power and transform it into something blessed. Strength without mercy is cruelty, and mercy without strength is weakness. Strength shows us the balance.

In numerology, the number 8 represents both material and spiritual power together. It's the number of infinity — ∞ — reminding us that true power is circular, not linear. When we give love, we receive love. When we give strength, we receive strength.

In the card sequence, Strength comes after the Hierophant who taught us about traditional wisdom, and before the Hermit who will teach us about the wisdom of solitude. This is a card that speaks of wisdom gained through active engagement with life — neither fleeing from it nor blindly obeying it.

practice2.instruction: Think of a situation in your life where you currently need more patience or self-control. Ask the card: 'How can I be strong with gentleness in this situation?' Draw an additional card for insight.

sections.deepDive:
What's not written in the books about Strength

The deepest secret of the Strength card is that it teaches us about a different kind of time — feminine time. In a world that runs on schedules and externalities and competition, Strength reminds us that there are things that cannot be rushed. There are processes that need to ripen in their own time.

The woman in the card doesn't compete with the lion or try to outsmart it. She is simply there, present, patient. She understands that subduing the lion by force would destroy it — and she doesn't want to destroy, she wants to create.

This is the card of women leading in male industries, of people dealing with addictions, of parents with teenage children. Anywhere that gentle strength is more effective than brute force. This card teaches us that resistance usually only strengthens what we resist — whereas acceptance and love can change everything.

questions.question: What number is the Strength card in the Major Arcana?
questions.explanation: Strength is card number 8 in the Major Arcana, representing balance between spiritual and material power.

questions.question: How does the woman in the card affect the lion?
questions.explanation: The woman gently opens the lion's mouth with love and tenderness, representing inner strength over physical force.

questions.question: Which astrological sign connects to the Strength card?
questions.explanation: Leo connects to the Strength card, creating an interesting paradox between personal power and self-control.

questions.question: What does the lion represent in the card?
questions.explanation: The lion represents our wild nature - anger, fear, desires - that need to be nurtured rather than suppressed.

questions.question: What central quality does the Strength card teach?
questions.explanation: The Strength card teaches self-control and inner power - the ability to master ourselves rather than others.

closing.summary: Strength is a card that invites us to recognize our true power — not that which is rooted in controlling others, but that which is rooted in the ability to work with our inner forces wisely and lovingly. The woman and the lion teach us that true courage is not fighting against our nature, but nurturing it.
closing.wisdom: The greatest strength is the power to choose our response.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll meet the Hermit — the inner journey into quiet wisdom...

faq.question: Why does the Strength card represent gentle courage rather than harsh courage?
faq.answer: Because true strength is not physical force or aggression, but the ability to control ourselves and respond with wisdom. The woman in the card shows that we can influence through gentleness and love.

faq.question: What's the connection between Strength and Leo?
faq.answer: Leo represents personal power and self-confidence, but the Strength card teaches that even the greatest power must be directed with wisdom and love, not aggression.

faq.question: How to use the Strength card when feeling weak?
faq.answer: Strength reminds us that weakness is sometimes actually the beginning of true power. When we acknowledge our weaknesses, we can start working with them instead of against them.

faq.question: What's the meaning of the number 8 in the Strength card?
faq.answer: 8 represents perfect balance and infinity. It's the number of power that constantly renews itself - the more power we give with wisdom and love, the more power we receive.

seo.title: Strength in Tarot | Gentle Power and Inner Courage - Maktuba
seo.description: Learn about the Strength card in Tarot - inner power, self-control and gentle courage. The complete guide to meanings, astrological and Kabbalistic connections.
